The Palala tree (Strychnos pungens) is a plant species native to South Africa, belonging to the Strychnos genus of flowering plants. The health benefits of Palala tree extracts include their potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to their use in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The bioactive constituents of the Palala tree include alkaloids, such as strychnine and brucine, as well as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The most common herbal preparations made with Palala tree extracts include teas and tinctures, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
